name: Inverse seesaw
summary: Adds two singlet fermions per generation (N_R, S) with Dirac mass m_D N H L between L and N_R, Dirac mass M between N_R and S, and small Majorana mass mu_S for S. Light neutrino masses m_nu ~ (m_D / M)^2 * mu_S; M can be at the TeV scale because suppression comes from the small lepton-number-breaking parameter mu_S, making the seesaw testable at colliders.

addresses_puzzles.note: Tradeoff vs. type-I: M can be at TeV (testable) but at the cost of an additional small parameter mu_S. The smallness of mu_S is technically natural ('t Hooft) since it breaks lepton number.

touches_benchmarks.note: Pseudo-Dirac structure suppresses 0nubb relative to canonical seesaw; searches in inverted-hierarchy m_betabeta region are key tests.
Heavy-light mixing |U|^2 ~ m_D^2 / M^2 ~ 10^{-3} for TeV-scale M produces observable LFV in current and next-generation experiments.

introduces.note: Three Dirac mass scales, three Majorana parameters mu_S, mixing matrix; the testable window is the M ~ TeV regime.

history: Mohapatra-Valle (1986); Bernabeu-Pilaftsis-Schechter-Valle (1987). Revived as a testable seesaw scenario through 2010s; ATLAS/CMS direct searches for heavy neutral leptons constrain the parameter space.

references.citation: Mohapatra and Valle, 'Neutrino mass and baryon-number nonconservation in superstring models', Phys. Rev. D 34, 1642 (1986)
